task_completion.description: Open the meal train schedule in LibreOffice Calc
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc is open and displaying the meal train dataset with columns for dates, volunteers, and meals.

task_completion.description: Identify the scheduling gap in the meal train
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 4-day gap between April 5 and April 9 is highlighted, flagged with a note, or explicitly identified in the spreadsheet.
The duplicate entries on April 12 (Linda Martinez and Tom Anderson) are highlighted, flagged, or noted as a conflict in the spreadsheet.

task_completion.description: Identify the dietary issue in the schedule
task_completion.visual_evidence: The entry on April 16 for 'Chicken noodle soup' (Patricia Davis) is highlighted, flagged, or noted as a dietary violation.

task_completion.description: Calculate the coverage statistics for the meal train
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section in the spreadsheet displays calculated statistics, such as total meals (18), total days in the range (25), or coverage percentage.

integrity.description: The agent used LibreOffice Calc to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Calc graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the spreadsheet cells to identify issues and calculate statistics
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible cell selections, formula bar usage, or formatting actions (like highlighting) in the LibreOffice Calc GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by writing a script to generate the output file directly
integrity.visual_evidence: The progress and modifications are visible step-by-step in the LibreOffice Calc window, not just a terminal executing a Python/bash script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 18 volunteer signups from April 1 to April 25, 2024 (25 days). The VLM must verify the agent identified three specific planted issues: 1) A 4-day gap between April 5 and April 9. 2) A duplicate scheduling conflict on April 12 (Linda Martinez and Tom Anderson). 3) A dietary violation on April 16 ('Chicken noodle soup' by Patricia Davis, violating the vegetarian requirement). There is also an off-peak delivery time on April 19 at 20:30. Coverage statistics should reflect the 18 meals over the 25-day period.
